name: Svava
description: Svava is a Valkyrie in Norse mythology, appearing primarily in the Poetic Edda poem Helgakviða Hjörvarðssonar. She is depicted as a beautiful and skilled warrior maiden, one of the shieldmaidens who serve Odin and choose who lives and dies on the battlefield. Svava is particularly associated with the hero Helgi Hjörvarðsson, whom she loves and protects. She gives him his name and ensures his success in battle. Her role extends beyond mere battle assistance; she embodies the ideal of the Valkyrie as both a fierce warrior and a figure of love and inspiration. While details about her origins are scarce, she is generally considered one of Odin's chosen, tasked with guiding worthy fallen warriors to Valhalla. Svava's powers include superhuman strength, agility, and the ability to influence the outcome of battles. She can also appear in dreams and visions to offer guidance or warnings. Though her appearances in Norse texts are limited, Svava represents the strength, loyalty, and protective nature of the Valkyries, serving as a powerful figure in the mythology surrounding heroic warriors and their destinies.
subtitle: Valkyrie, protector of heroes

keyMyths.title: Naming of Helgi
keyMyths.description: Svava appears to Helgi's father in a dream and instructs him to name the boy Helgi and to give him a spear and shield.

keyMyths.title: Svava's Protection
keyMyths.description: Svava protects Helgi in battle, ensuring his victory against his enemies through her influence and skill.

keyMyths.title: Svava's Love
keyMyths.description: Svava's love for Helgi is a central theme, showcasing the Valkyries' capacity for deep and meaningful relationships with mortal heroes.

sources.author: Henry Adams Bellows
